Title: Group/DM message mutation over-permission
Steps: 1) Sign in as non-sender participant (group member or DM receiver). 2) Run update on messages body/deleted for message not authored by self.
Expected: Only sender can edit/delete body; receiver only mark read.
Actual: Update policy allows broad updates for receivers/group members.
RootCauseHypothesis: RLS update policy is role-wide and not column-scoped.
FixRecommendation: Split policies: sender-only edit/delete body fields; receiver-only read flag update; optionally use RPC for controlled updates.

Title: Parents can read report comments beyond linked children
Steps: 1) Sign in as Parent A. 2) Query report_comments table directly.
Expected: Parent should only see comments for linked students.
Actual: Policy grants select to any user with parent role.
FixRecommendation: Update report_comments_select to join parent_students on student_id for parent role.

Title: Overly broad profile data exposure
Steps: 1) Sign in as authenticated user. 2) Query profiles with select *.
Expected: Users should see limited fields or only relevant profiles.
Actual: Any authenticated user can read full profile rows.
FixRecommendation: Narrow profile select policy and create a safe view for public profile fields.

Title: Global activity log readable by all users
Steps: 1) Sign in as any non-admin user. 2) Query activity_log.
Expected: Activity log should be admin/ops-visible only.
RootCauseHypothesis: Permissive read policy without role check.
FixRecommendation: Restrict activity_log_select to admin role; optionally teacher scoped logs.

Title: Potential privacy leak via public bucket
Steps: 1) Upload message/course attachment. 2) Access public URL unauthenticated.
Expected: Sensitive attachments should require auth or signed URL.
FixRecommendation: Split buckets by sensitivity or use private bucket + signed URLs for private assets.

Title: No explicit start-time block in server scoring route
Steps: 1) Submit before scheduled start if UI bypassed. 2) Observe API behavior.
Expected: Submission should be rejected before start window.
Actual: Route computes scheduledStart but only checks end deadline.
FixRecommendation: Add early-submission check with 403 and clear error reason.

Title: message_groups realtime dependency not fully documented in SQL guidance
Steps: 1) Rely only on documented replication toggles. 2) Create/update message group. 3) Observe sidebar sync.
Expected: Thread list should update consistently for group mutations.
Actual: Messaging listens to message_groups changes, but replication guidance does not mention message_groups.
RootCauseHypothesis: Drift between frontend realtime listeners and migration guidance.
FixRecommendation: Add publication for message_groups and update comments/checklist in SQL deployment notes.

Title: Weak parent-child linking trust model
Steps: 1) Enter known student QGX ID in parent dashboard. 2) Link child.
Actual: Link can occur directly by QGX ID knowledge.
RootCauseHypothesis: No challenge/approval flow in linking logic.
FixRecommendation: Introduce one-time parent link codes generated by student/admin + expiry + audit trail.

Title: Password reset links returned in API response
Steps: 1) Admin calls batch-create endpoint. 2) Observe resetLink in response payload.
Expected: Sensitive links should be handled securely with minimal exposure.
Actual: Endpoint returns action link directly to client.
FixRecommendation: Restrict visibility, short expiry, audit log retrieval, and optional email-only delivery.

Title: In-memory rate limiting not multi-instance safe
Steps: 1) Run multiple app instances or cold starts. 2) Send rapid AI requests.
Expected: Rate limits should be consistent across instances.
Actual: Limit can be bypassed across instances/restarts.
FixRecommendation: Use persistent/distributed limiter (Redis, Upstash, Supabase table with TTL).

Title: Parent role color mapping missing in sidebar badge
Steps: 1) Open parent dashboard. 2) Inspect role badge style.
Expected: Parent role should have explicit color mapping.
Actual: Parent role may fall back to undefined color token.
FixRecommendation: Add parent mapping in DashboardLayout roleColor record.

Title: Multiple img warnings from Next lint
Steps: 1) Run npm run lint. 2) Review no-img-element warnings.
Expected: Critical images should use next/image for optimization.
Actual: Raw img elements remain in several components.
FixRecommendation: Replace with next/image where feasible and add intentional exceptions where not feasible.

Title: TypeScript version outside ts-estree supported range
Steps: 1) Run npm run lint. 2) Read warning banner.
Expected: Toolchain versions should be aligned to supported matrix.
Actual: TypeScript 5.9.3 is above declared supported range for parser warning.
FixRecommendation: Pin compatible TS or upgrade eslint/typescript-estree stack together.

Title: Any authenticated user can insert announcements
Steps: 1) Authenticated non-admin/non-teacher inserts announcement directly. 2) Query announcements.
Expected: Only authorized publisher roles should insert.
Actual: Policy allows insert for any authenticated user.
FixRecommendation: Restrict insert policy to admin/teacher and enforce target constraints by role.
